channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Hierarchical Latent Structures in Data Generation Process Unify Mechanistic Phenomena across Scale
item.description: arXiv:2603.06592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Contemporary studies have uncovered many puzzling phenomena in the neural information processing of Transformer-based language models. Building a robust, unified understanding of these phenomena requires disassembling a model within the scope of its t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, augmented, LLM, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Rethinking Personalization in Large Language Models at the Token Level
item.description: arXiv:2603.06595v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With large language models (LLMs) now performing strongly across diverse tasks, there is growing demand for them to personalize outputs for individual users. Personalization is typically framed as an additional layer on top of a base NLP task, requiri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, LLM, large language model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: "Dark Triad" Model Organisms of Misalignment: Narrow Fine-Tuning Mirrors Human Antisocial Behavior
item.description: arXiv:2603.06816v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The alignment problem refers to concerns regarding powerful intelligences, ensuring compatibility with human preferences and values as capabilities increase. Current large language models (LLMs) show misaligned behaviors, such as strategic deception, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, model, fine-tuning, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Validation of a Small Language Model for DSM-5 Substance Category Classification in Child Welfare Records
item.description: arXiv:2603.06836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background: Recent studies have demonstrated that large language models (LLMs) can perform binary classification tasks on child welfare narratives, detecting the presence or absence of constructs such as substance-related problems, domestic violence, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Language Shapes Mental Health Evaluations in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.06910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates whether large language models (LLMs) exhibit cross-linguistic differences in mental health evaluations. Focusing on Chinese and English, we examine two widely used models, GPT-4o and Qwen3, to assess whether prompt language sys...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, model, LLM, large language model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scaling Strategy, Not Compute: A Stand-Alone, Open-Source StarCraft II Benchmark for Accessible Reinforcement Learning Research
item.description: arXiv:2603.06608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The research community lacks a middle ground between StarCraft IIs full game and its mini-games. The full-games sprawling state-action space renders reward signals sparse and noisy, but in mini-games simple agents saturate performance. This complexity...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, experiment, arxiv, research, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Symmetry-Constrained Language-Guided Program Synthesis for Discovering Governing Equations from Noisy and Partial Observations
item.description: arXiv:2603.06869v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Discovering compact governing equations from experimental observations is one of the defining objectives of quantitative science, yet practical discovery pipelines routinely fail when measurements are noisy, relevant state variables are unobserved, or...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, framework, RAG, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Khatri-Rao Clustering for Data Summarization
item.description: arXiv:2603.06602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As datasets continue to grow in size and complexity, finding succinct yet accurate data summaries poses a key challenge. Centroid-based clustering, a widely adopted approach to address this challenge, finds informative summaries of datasets in terms o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, study, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Breaking the Martingale Curse: Multi-Agent Debate via Asymmetric Cognitive Potential Energy
item.description: arXiv:2603.06801v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-Agent Debate (MAD) has emerged as a promising paradigm for enhancing large language model reasoning. However, recent work reveals a limitation:standard MAD cannot improve belief correctness beyond majority voting; we refer to this as the Marting...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, large language model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FuzzingRL: Reinforcement Fuzz-Testing for Revealing VLM Failures
item.description: arXiv:2603.06600v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision Language Models (VLMs) are prone to errors, and identifying where these errors occur is critical for ensuring the reliability and safety of AI systems. In this paper, we propose an approach that automatically generates questions designed to del...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, vision, example, model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Counting on Consensus: Selecting the Right Inter-annotator Agreement Metric for NLP Annotation and Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.06865v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human annotation remains the foundation of reliable and interpretable data in Natural Language Processing (NLP). As annotation and evaluation tasks continue to expand, from categorical labelling to segmentation, subjective judgment, and continuous rat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
